name: Glenwoodie Golf Club
about: Glenwoodie Golf Club is an 18-hole, par 72 championship course located in Glenwood, Illinois. Consistently ranked among the top 35 public courses, the facility features narrow bentgrass fairways, large rolling greens, and is surrounded by groves of majestic oak trees. The 16th hole is noted as one of the best public golf holes in Chicagoland. Owned by the Village of Glenwood and internally operated since 1999, the venue offers a championship experience for both serious and recreational players. The property includes a golf simulator, multiple event spaces for weddings and corporate functions, and instructional programs through The First Tee for juniors.
why_golfers_like_it: Championship-caliber course ranked among the top 35 public courses
Comprehensive event hosting capabilities for weddings and corporate outings
Youth and instructional programs through The First Tee
Convenient location 25 minutes south of downtown Chicago

food_and_drink.notes: On-site banquet facility with kitchen service for events
No mention of alcohol service on premises

simulator_hardware.notes: Specific simulator hardware details not provided on website

faq.question: Do I need to bring my own clubs?
faq.answer: The website does not specify club rental options, so players should bring their own equipment.

faq.question: Can I bring my own food and drinks?
faq.answer: No, the venue provides food service for events, but there is no mention of outside food or drink policies.

faq.question: How long does it typically take to play 18 holes?
faq.answer: Standard 18-hole play time is not specified, but the facility offers tee time booking through their online system.

faq.question: Is this venue kid-friendly?
faq.answer: Yes, the venue hosts The First Tee programs for kids and teens aged 7-18, offering affordable after-school programs and summer camps.
